events.title: Global famine fears rise as Hormuz crisis threatens ‘eight-year,' Suez-scale disruption
events.description: Global famine fears are rising as the Strait of Hormuz crisis strains food supply chains, drives up fertilizer prices and threatens harvests worldwide.

events.title: Britain warns another terror attack is 'highly likely' within 6 months after London stabbing
events.description: Britain raised its national terror threat level to severe after two people were stabbed in an antisemitic attack in London's Golders Green neighborhood.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Britain warns another terror attack is 'highly likely' within 6 months after London stabbing

events.title: Jefferson Lewis charged with murder over death of Kumanjayi Little Baby near Alice Springs
events.description: Warlpiri girl went missing on Saturday 25 April from a town camp and was found dead five days later Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Northern Territory police have charged Jefferson Lewis with murder over the death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby. The Warlpiri girl went missing on Saturday 25 April from her bed in a town camp near Alice Springs. Indigenous Australians can call 13YARN on 13 92 76 for information and crisis support; or call Lifeline on 13 11 14 or Mensline on 1300 789 978 Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jefferson Lewis charged with murder over death of Kumanjayi Little Baby near Alice Springs

events.title: Mali in crisis: Jihadist fighters and Tuareg separatists threaten Bamako
events.description: As Mali’s military struggles to maintain control amid a growing jihadist insurgency, a number of its own soldiers have been arrested, accused of collaborating with Al-Qaeda-linked militants in coordinating widespread attacks. Islamist group JNIM, alongside separatist rebels, has reportedly seized two northern cities, killed the defence minister, and is now attempting to impose a blockade on the capital, Bamako. We take a closer look at what’s happening on the ground, the escalating security crisis, and the role of Russian mercenaries in supporting the government. France 24’s Gavin Lee is joined by Beverley Ochieng, a specialist in West Africa and political risk analyst at Control Risks, to discuss.

events.title: World News in Brief: Displacement in South Sudan, fraud centres in the Philippines, new migration patterns in Latin America
events.description: In South Sudan, the UN aid coordination office [OCHA] reported on Thursday that conflict and flooding continue to drive displacement and food insecurity higher throughout the country.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ World News in Brief: Displacement in South Sudan, fraud centres in the Philippines, new migration patterns in Latin America

events.title: Nuclear fears resurface among younger generations amid global tensions
events.description: The threat of nuclear Armageddon, a constant worry for many who grew up during the tense decades of the Cold War, is becoming a cause for concern for a growing number of young people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nuclear fears resurface among younger generations amid global tensions

events.title: Iran’s killer drones increase slaughter in Sudan amid world’s forgotten war
events.description: Iran is accused of supplying Mohajer-6 attack drones to Sudan's armed forces as the civil war's death toll reportedly reaches as many as 400,000 people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s killer drones increase slaughter in Sudan amid world’s forgotten war

events.title: New Iranian proposal to end the war under examination
events.description: A senior Iranian officer on Saturday said that fresh fighting with the United States is “likely”, underscoring rising tensions after President Donald Trump dismissed Tehran’s latest negotiating proposal and issued fresh threats to attack the country. Daniel Quinlan reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Iranian proposal to end the war under examination

events.title: Middle East crisis puts aid, food, fuel further out of reach for millions already struggling – UN agencies
events.description: As the Middle East crisis continues the humanitarian fallout is worsening, with aid route disruptions and food and fuel price hikes wrecking the lives and the rights of the most vulnerable people worldwide, UN agencies warned on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East crisis puts aid, food, fuel further out of reach for millions already struggling – UN agencies

events.title: Hormuz crisis strangling global economy, Guterres warns, demanding solutions to end stalemate
events.description: The escalating crisis in the Strait of Hormuz could push tens of millions into poverty, trigger a surge in global hunger and even tip the world towards recession, the UN Secretary-General warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hormuz crisis strangling global economy, Guterres warns, demanding solutions to end stalemate

events.title: Spotlight on the continued decline of press freedom globally
events.description: Press freedom has fallen to its lowest level in 25 years. That warning comes from Reporters Without Borders — also known as RSF — which released its annual index ahead of World Press Freedom Day this Sunday. To mark the occasion, one of RSF’s partners, Cartooning for Peace, has released a series of short animated films based on cartoons by three artists from its global network, including work by Russian cartoonist Mikhail Zlatkovsky, Canadian cartoonist Serge Chapleau Coté, and Turkish artist Zehra Doğan. Thibaut Bruttin, Director General of Reporters Without Borders, speaks to France 24 about press freedom nowadays.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Spotlight on the continued decline of press freedom globally

events.title: 2015 nuclear deal ‘no basis’ for any new agreement with Iran
events.description: The 2015 nuclear accord with Iran cannot be the starting point for a new agreement with the country, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said on Wednesday in New York.

events.title: Weather tracker: Tornadoes and giant hail in central US
events.description: Strongest tornado hits Mineral Wells, Texas, where disaster was declared. Elsewhere, extreme rain inundates China Spring is the season for severe thunderstorms across the central US, and the start of this week was a particularly active period for the region. A favourable weather pattern fuelled intense thunderstorms on Monday through Wednesday, bringing strong winds, very large hail and strong tornadoes. Eight tornadoes were reported on Monday, including an EF2 tornado that ripped through the town of Sycamore, Kansas. On Tuesday, a more widespread event tore across the mid-west, most notably as a severe hailstorm moved through Springfield, Missouri. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Tornadoes and giant hail in central US

events.title: Global demining work strained by rising conflicts and shrinking aid
events.description: Demining experts from around the world have been sharing their collective shock at the widespread and growing threat from unexploded ordnance, the new head of the UN Mine Action Service (UNMAS) said this week.

events.title: Cuba's economy collapses, prices soar
events.description: Many Cubans who lived through the Special Period of the 1990s, when Soviet support collapsed, say the current crisis is even worse. Cuba is facing a deep economic and energy emergency, with daily life becoming increasingly difficult across the island. Because Cuba imports up to 80% of the food it consumes, essential goods are becoming harder and harder to find. Gabrielle Nadler reports.
events.ticker_label: 📈 Cuba's economy collapses, prices soar

events.title: Drone kills 2 in Kherson minibus strike, as Russia claims front-line progress
events.description: A Russian drone attack on a minibus in Kherson, Ukraine, has killed two people and injured seven, according to local officials
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone kills 2 in Kherson minibus strike, as Russia claims front-line progress

events.title: Ukraine battlefield: Advanced Ukrainian drones raise concerns among Russian forces
events.description: From Ukraine’s evolving battlefield, where drone warfare is redefining frontlines and so-called “killing zones,” to reports of “Martian drones” and their impact on both Russian and Ukrainian forces, we also look at the expanding use of unmanned systems in rescue operations, including the evacuation of a 77-year-old woman in Odesa region. France 24’s Gavin Lee is joined by Olena Krizhanivska, a Ukrainian defence analyst specialising in drones and unmanned systems, and founder of Ukraine’s Arms Monitor.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine battlefield: Advanced Ukrainian drones raise concerns among Russian forces

events.title: Paramilitary forces drone strike kills 5 near Sudan capital, rights group says
events.description: Sudan's paramilitary group, the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), has killed at least five people in a drone attack, according to a local rights group
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Paramilitary forces drone strike kills 5 near Sudan capital, rights group says

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 1 May: Strain on food, fuel and aid systems continues
events.description: The strain on food, fuel and aid systems worldwide continues as the Middle East crisis deepens. Rising oil prices, supply disruptions and mounting transport costs are affecting humanitarian operations and access to essential goods far beyond the region. Stay with us this Friday for live updates.

events.title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism
events.description: Two men were stabbed in north London's Barnet borough, prompting a counterterrorism-led investigation into a potentially antisemitic incident.
events.ticker_label: 💥 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism

events.title: Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany
events.description: Media tycoon honoured in absentia as critics decry his 20-year sentence under national security law The jailed media entrepreneur Jimmy Lai has been awarded Deutsche Welle’s freedom of speech award for his contribution to Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement. The German public broadcaster said on Thursday that Lai would be presented in absentia with the 12th iteration of the award on 23 June at the DW Global Media Forum in Bonn. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany

events.title: Elderly Ukrainian woman rescued using robotic vehicle
events.description: Ukrainian forces have attacked two tankers from Russia’s so-called shadow fleet at the entrance to the Port of Novorossiysk on the Black Sea. The announcement was made by Volodymyr Zelensky on the Telegram messaging app. He added that Ukraine would continue to use long-range weapons to strike targets at sea, on land and in the air. Meanwhile, in the Donetsk Oblast, an elderly woman was rescued using an unmanned ground vehicle. Gabrielle Nadler has the story.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Elderly Ukrainian woman rescued using robotic vehicle

events.title: Gang-controlled streets, shuttered newsrooms: How violence is eroding Haiti’s media
events.description: Journalists working in Haiti are under constant threat of death or injury from rapidly expanding criminal gangs, as they continue to report news and information which they hope will help keep fellow citizens safe.

events.title: Lebanon strikes deepen crisis as hunger rises and conditions worsen in Gaza
events.description: At least nine people were reported to have been killed in southern Lebanon on Thursday, as ongoing hostilities continue to exact a heavy toll on civilians and drive a worsening humanitarian crisis marked by rising hunger and strained public services.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon strikes deepen crisis as hunger rises and conditions worsen in Gaza

events.title: Man charged with murder and sexual assault of 5-year-old Australian girl
events.description: The girl's death sparked an outbreak of violence in the central Australian town from where she disappeared.

events.title: BAE faces £120m lawsuit over decision to scrap support for aid aircraft
events.description: EnComm Aviation says the firm’s action has cut off vital support for crisis-hit countries including South Sudan and the DRC Britain’s biggest weapons manufacturer, BAE Systems, is facing a £120m lawsuit after scrapping support for aircraft used to deliver aid to some of the world’s neediest countries. EnComm Aviation, a Kenya-based aid cargo operator, claims the decision forced the cancellation of humanitarian contracts and reduced supplies to South Sudan, now threatened by famine , Somalia and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), among others. Continue reading...

events.title: Attacks on media workers must end, UN urges
events.description: War in the Middle East has made Lebanon the deadliest country for media workers so far this year, but practically no country offers a safe environment in which to be a journalist, according to the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Attacks on media workers must end, UN urges

events.title: DPRK Korea: Continued militarisation a ‘serious concern’, political affairs chief warns Security Council
events.description: The continued pursuit of nuclear weapons and ballistic missile development by The Democratic People’s Republic of Korea remain “a matter of serious concern,” the UN’s political affairs chief told the Security Council on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 DPRK Korea: Continued militarisation a ‘serious concern’, political affairs chief warns Security Council

events.title: Israel issues new forced displacement orders in southern Lebanon
events.description: Warning covers more than 10 villages and towns, including several in Nabatieh district that lie north of Litani River.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel issues new forced displacement orders in southern Lebanon

events.title: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
events.description: Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’ The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”. Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May, but Thabo Kawana, permanent secretary for the Ministry of Information & Media, said last week that the conference would not go ahead to allow time to ensure the gathering “aligns with Zambia’s national values, policy priorities, and broader public interest considerations”. Continue reading...

events.title: World News in Brief: Aung San Suu Kyi released from prison, Israel’s new death penalty, Cambodia convictions, healthcare inequalities in South America
events.description: Myanmar State Counsellor Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, with her sentence reduced under a prisoner amnesty linked to a Buddhist holiday.

events.title: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
events.description: Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy future Looking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. Oil tankers ride at anchor on the horizon and sometimes, locals say, lumps of coal wash up on the shore, blown off the collier ships that carry cargos from the nearby mines. It was here, on Wednesday evening, that the Colombian government took a bold step to shift its economy – and that of the rest of the world – away from dependence on coal, gas and oil and into a new era of clean energy. With the first ever conference on “transitioning away from fossil fuels”, the host joined nearly 60 countries determined to loosen of the grip of petrostates on the world’s future. Continue reading...

events.title: Student’s alleged jailing in China over Australian pro-democracy protests sparks calls for inquiry
events.description: Human rights commissioner says alleged jailing highlights the ‘growing risks of transnational repression’ in Australia Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s human rights commissioner has said the Chinese student who was allegedly jailed for six years by Chinese authorities for joining protests in Sydney underscores the “very real and growing risks of transnational repression affecting people in Australia – including international students”. Commissioner Lorraine Finlay told Guardian Australia that while she could not comment on the circumstances of individual cases “no one should fear punishment abroad for exercising their lawful rights to free expression and peaceful protest here”. Continue reading...

events.title: Dozens paddle out in Sydney and Newcastle in solidarity with Gaza Flotilla – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Canavan defends Coalition decision to preference One Nation ahead of independent in Farrer: ‘Socialists and communists always go last’ Matt Canavan said he understood why voters in Farrer, set for a byelection next Saturday, were disillusioned with the federal opposition. I‘ve been down there for 20 days, 15 nights – and 10 of those I’ve been in a swag – over the past month. I’m doing everything I can to fight for them. I’d love to fight for that great part of our country again, it’s a wonderful part of our nation where we pioneered the use of irrigation and dams. It still produces 40% of our food, 60% of our fruit. Under my leadership, [in] my view, socialists and communists always go last. I think we need to really see how the overseas conflict evolves, how prices evolve for oil. But I think, you know, it is doing what it was intended to do. It’s not a silver bullet, but it is providing material support for people. And as I said, it’s providing support for people who are really doing it tough when they can’t shift away from using petrol. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 📈 Dozens paddle out in Sydney and Newcastle in solidarity with Gaza Flotilla – as it happened

events.title: 'We cannot give up': Hong Kong journalists navigate fear, surveillance and shrinking space
events.description: Hong Kong’s government on Friday slammed foreign media and press freedom groups, rejecting claims of a crackdown on press freedom as “slander” after jailed media tycoon Jimmy Lai was awarded a free speech prize in Germany. Press freedom in the city has sharply declined since a 2020 National Security Law clamped down on dissent. Journalists face visa denials, surveillance, self-censorship and legal threats, while independent outlets struggle to survive.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'We cannot give up': Hong Kong journalists navigate fear, surveillance and shrinking space

events.title: Activists’ detention deepens Spain-Israel tensions as aid group says men have begun hunger strike
events.description: Spain’s Foreign Minister José Manuel Albares has demanded the immediate release of a Spanish-Swedish activist detained by Israel after an aid flotilla bound for Gaza was intercepted in international waters
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Activists’ detention deepens Spain-Israel tensions as aid group says men have begun hunger strike

events.title: Abuse of women journalists made ‘easier and more damaging’ by AI
events.description: Reports of online violence against women journalists have doubled since 2020, with serious impacts on their health and well-being, according to a study published ahead of World Press Freedom Day marked annually on 3 May.

events.title: Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report
events.description: Human rights groups allege Russia recruits tens of thousands of vulnerable migrants from over 130 countries to fight in its ongoing war against Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report

events.title: Nigeria summons South African envoy over attacks on its nationals
events.description: There has been a wave of anti-migrant protests in South Africa, some of which have turned violent.

events.title: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
events.description: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?

events.title: South Korea holds pro-Palestine protest in solidarity with Gaza
events.description: Protesters in South Korea’s capital, Seoul, were in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza enduring genocidal attacks.

events.title: Anti-Christian aggressions on the rise in Jerusalem
events.description: Religious groups have documented a rise in harassment and violence against Christians in parts of Israel. One of the main flashpoints is Israeli-occupied East Jerusalem. The Old City of Jerusalem is home to some of the holiest sites in the world for Jews, Christians and Muslims.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Anti-Christian aggressions on the rise in Jerusalem

events.title: 'Killing off the country': Iran executes dozens, arrests 4,000-plus in war crackdown
events.description: Iran is being accused of intensifying its crackdown on dissent with executions, mass arrests and one of the internet longest shutdowns to stifle protests against the regime.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Killing off the country': Iran executes dozens, arrests 4,000-plus in war crackdown

events.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
events.description: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says

events.title: First malaria drug for babies is approved in ‘major public health milestone’
events.description: WHO prequalification of Coartem Baby means newborns can be safely treated rather than using medication for older children The first malaria treatment for babies has been approved by the World Health Organization, opening the door to widespread use around the globe. In parts of Africa, up to 18% of children under six months will be infected with malaria, but there has historically been no safe treatment for the smallest of them. There were 610,000 deaths from malaria in 2024, about three quarters of which were under-fives in Africa. Continue reading...

events.title: World News in Brief: Europe warming twice as fast, acute hunger in Lebanon, peacekeepers deployed in Congo
events.description: New data shows record heatwaves from the Mediterranean to the Arctic last year, with glaciers shrinking and snow cover declining, according to the UN World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Europe warming twice as fast, acute hunger in Lebanon, peacekeepers deployed in Congo

events.title: Court sentences former world leader to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest, other charges
events.description: Ousted South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to seven years in prison after martial law decree that triggered a severe political crisis.

events.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
events.description: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release

events.title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
events.description: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss

events.title: Missing Oscar found after Academy Award winner blocked from taking it on flight
events.description: The airline said the Academy Award was safely in its care in Frankfurt and was arranging its return.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Missing Oscar found after Academy Award winner blocked from taking it on flight

events.title: Imprisoned Iranian Nobel laureate Mohammadi rushed to hospital following cardiac 'crisis'
events.description: Iran’s imprisoned Nobel peace prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to hospital to receive urgent care after experiencing a “catastrophic deterioration” of her health, a foundation run by her family said on Saturday. Mohammadi was moved on Friday after suffering a heart attack and experiencing two episodes of “complete” unconsciousness, it said.

events.title: Central Asia celebrates 20 years as a nuclear-weapon-free zone
events.description: Semipalatinsk, Kazakhstan was once the Soviet Union’s primary testing ground for nuclear weapons. Today, in an age of rising nuclear threats, the Semipalatinsk Treaty – which saw a group of Central Asian countries renounce nuclear weapons in 2006 – is more relevant than ever.

events.title: Missing man’s body found in abandoned cemetery crypt in case tied to biker gang
events.description: Investigators searching for missing musician Keith Palumbo found his body sealed inside a cemetery crypt in a case involving the Warlocks MC. His case is being explored in the true crime series "Philly Homicide."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Missing man’s body found in abandoned cemetery crypt in case tied to biker gang

events.title: China blocks US sanctions against five ‘teapot’ refineries
events.description: Ministry of Commerce says sanctions against refineries accused of importing Iranian oil violate international law.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Pentagon announces withdrawal of 5.000 soldiers from Germany
events.description: The US will pull around 5,000 of its troops from Germany over the next year after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said that Tehran was "humiliating" Washington. NATO said Saturday that it is working with the US to understand the details of the decision. France 24 International Affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Pentagon announces withdrawal of 5.000 soldiers from Germany

events.title: Israel detains two Gaza activists after intercepting aid flotilla in international waters
events.description: Israel on Saturday said it had brought two Gaza flotilla activists in for questioning after having intercepted the aid operation in international waters Thursday. Spain slammed the detention of one of its nationals as “illegal”.

events.title: Bus plunges into river after trainee driver crash, massive rescue response: reports
events.description: All four people on board were rescued after a trainee driver sent a bus careening into the River Seine near Paris Thursday, officials said.

events.title: Mother-in-law of slain beauty queen arrested after international manhunt
events.description: Authorities arrested the suspected fugitive mother-in-law accused of killing a former Mexican beauty queen after a two-week international manhunt.

events.title: Teen suspect in murder of two bank employees in Kentucky leads police on wild 130 mph chase
events.description: An 18-year-old suspect faces federal charges, including armed bank robbery, after allegedly shooting two employees at a Kentucky U.S. Bank branch.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Teen suspect in murder of two bank employees in Kentucky leads police on wild 130 mph chase

events.title: Rubio warns China after Panama ship detentions, calls hemisphere sovereignty 'non-negotiable'
events.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio warns hemisphere sovereignty is "non-negotiable" as U.S. and allies condemn China's reported detention of Panama ships.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Rubio warns China after Panama ship detentions, calls hemisphere sovereignty 'non-negotiable'

events.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
events.description: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war

events.description: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

events.description: Ukrainian forces also strike two shadow fleet tankers near port of Novorossiysk, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says.

events.title: Millions of jobs lost as Iranians battle ‘Operation Economic Fury’
events.description: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is crippling life in Tehran.

events.title: Trump expresses doubt that Iran’s peace proposal is ‘acceptable’
events.description: US president says he is considering the 14-point peace plan, but warns strikes could resume if Tehran 'misbehaves'.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump expresses doubt that Iran’s peace proposal is ‘acceptable’

events.title: Inflation surging past 50% in Iran
events.description: The fragile ceasefire may have paused the fighting, but the economic fallout in Iran continues. A US naval blockade of Iranian ports has sharply reduced the Iran’s oil exports, while the cost of everyday goods has continued to rise. Across the country, people are now bracing for further price increases and growing job losses. Liza Kaminov reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Inflation surging past 50% in Iran

events.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised
events.description: Iran’s imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been urgently transferred from prison to hospital after what her foundation described as a severe deterioration in her health. She reportedly fainted twice on Friday. The incident follows a suspected heart attack in late March, raising growing concern among her supporters over her worsening condition.

events.title: Man charged with murder of five-year-old girl whose death sparked riot
events.description: A man has been charged with murdering a five-year-old girl in central Australia, days after her killing sparked a riot.

events.title: Passengers pulled from water after learner driver crashes bus into major European river

events.title: Activist remains perched atop DC bridge protesting Iran war, AI development: 'The spirit moves me'

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: 2 dead and 16 injured in attempted channel crossing from France to UK
events.description: A small boat carrying migrants trying to cross the English Channel has run aground on a beach in northern France, leaving two dead and 16 injured

events.description: Four noncitizens in New Jersey were charged with illegally voting in the 2020 and 2024 presidential elections and making false citizenship statements.

events.title: Female inmate sues Washington state after alleged attack by male-born prisoner in women's facility
events.description: A female Washington inmate is suing corrections officials, alleging a male-born prisoner attacked her under the state's gender identity housing policy.

events.title: ‘No credibility’: Obama’s top Iran negotiator torched by State Department after ripping Trump war plan
events.description: The State Department pushes back on former Obama Iran deal negotiator Wendy Sherman after she criticizes Trump’s strategy, igniting a sharp war of words over Democrats' foreign policy with regime.

events.title: Sabastian Sawe receives hero’s welcome in Kenya after sub-two hour marathon feat
events.description: Record-breaker says London Marathon win was ‘a victory for all of us’ as he is greeted by family and friends in Eldoret Hugged, cheered and adorned with garlands, the first man to run an official marathon in under two hours has returned as a hero to his home village in Kenya. Sabastian Sawe, who stunned the world when he clocked 1h 59m 30s in the London Marathon last weekend, flew in a Kenyan military plane normally reserved for special operations on Thursday to his home region of western Kenya. events.title: Pope appoints former undocumented immigrant as bishop of West Virginia
events.description: Leo, who has criticized Trump’s hardline immigration policy, selected Evelio Menjivar-Ayala as state’s new bishop Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Pope Leo XIV has appointed a man who had once entered the United States as an undocumented immigrant, hidden in the trunk of a car, as the new bishop of West Virginia . The pope approved the resignation of Bishop Mark E Brennan of Wheeling-Charleston, West Virginia, and selected Bishop Evelio Menjivar-Ayala, 55, of Washington to take his place, reported OSV News . Continue reading...

events.title: Man charged with murdering three family members and attempting to murder fourth at Sydney home
events.description: Man, 32, due to appear in court on Monday over incident at Rosemeadow in early hours of Sunday morning Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man has been charged with three counts of murder and one of attempted murder after allegedly attacking his family members in a Sydney home. New South Wales police arrived at the house in Rosemeadow in Sydney’s south-west on Sunday just after 1.30am after reports of an assault. This article was updated on 3 May 2026 to correct the age of one of the men who died after clarification from NSW police. Continue reading...

Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to appeal against his guilty pleas, claiming harsh prison conditions had affected his mental health and compelled him to admit to the crimes. events.description: The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.

events.title: New Netflix documentary reexamines Winnie Mandela's divisive legacy
events.description: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.

events.title: After Assad's fall, Syria's Kurds are left in limbo, feeling abandoned by the U.S.
events.description: Caught in limbo after the fall of Syria's Bashar al-Assad, Kurdish families struggle with cold, loss and uncertainty — feeling abandoned by the U.S. allies they once fought alongside.

events.title: Germany news: Majority thinks coalition government doomed — survey
events.description: More than half of people in Germany believe the coalition government will collapse before its term is up. The head of a leading economic think tank says US tariffs could trigger a German recession. DW has more.

events.title: Austria: Police arrest suspect over poisoned HiPP baby food
events.description: A 39-year-old man has been arrested in Austria for his alleged involvement in an attempt to blackmail a German baby food manufacturer.

events.title: Mexican governor indicted by US steps down
events.description: Sinaloa Governor Ruben Rocha Moya has been accused of protecting the powerful Sinaloa cartel and helping it smuggle drugs into the US, allegedly taking in millions of dollars in bribes.

events.title: Why Ukraine and Israel are arguing over grain
events.description: Cargo ships have docked in Haifa, Israel, carrying grain thought to originate from Russian-occupied parts of Ukraine. Kyiv considers the cargo to be stolen goods — but Israel says evidence is lacking.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Why Ukraine and Israel are arguing over grain

events.title: US to withdraw thousands of troops from Germany
events.description: The decision to cut US troops comes amid rising tensions after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US was being "humiliated" by Iran's leadership.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US to withdraw thousands of troops from Germany

events.title: Kosovo to hold snap elections in June
events.description: Kosovo will soon hold its third parliamentary election in a year and a half. Prime Minister Albin Kurti was able to form a government in February, but now there's political deadlock over who should be elected president.

events.title: Humpback whale Timmy released in North Sea but risk remains
events.description: After weeks of drama, the stranded humpback whale is back in his natural habitat, but still not out of harm's way.

events.title: Two women die on migrant boat attempting to reach the UK from France
events.description: Two young women died on Sunday while trying to cross from northern France to the UK in a small boat carrying some 82 people, a French government official said. French officials say that the number of people reaching Britain from France has dropped significantly this year compared with 2025.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two women die on migrant boat attempting to reach the UK from France

events.title: PSG held to a draw by Lorient ahead of Bayern Munich Champions League clash
events.description: PSG did not prepare in the best possible way for their Champions League semi-final second leg against Bayern Munich on Wednesday. With a reshuffled squad, PSG were held to a 2-2 draw at home by Lorient. The result had no impact on the title race, as Lens also failed to win at Nice (1-1) and remain six points behind PSG.

events.title: Ex F1 driver and Paralympic champion dies 'suddenly'
events.description: Former Formula 1 driver Alex Zanardi has died at the age of 59.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ex F1 driver and Paralympic champion dies 'suddenly'

events.title: EU hits back at 'unacceptable' Donald Trump over plans to increase tariffs
events.description: The European Union's trade chair has hit back at Donald Trump after he announced plans to increase tariffs on vehicles exported to the US.

events.title: Fears for British couple detained in Iranian prison after inmates are executed
events.description: A British couple detained in Iran fear for their lives following the routine executions of fellow inmates and brutal fights regularly breaking out in the prison where they are being held.

events.title: Drug kingpin jailed after extradition from Dubai
events.description: A gangster who played a "key" role in a multi-million-pound drugs operation has been jailed for six years after being extradited to Scotland from the United Arab Emirates.

events.title: Suspect detained after rat poison found in baby food jars on supermarket shelves
events.description: Police in eastern Austria say a 39-year-old suspect has been arrested after rat poison turned up in some HiPP baby food jars on supermarket shelves in central Europe

events.title: Cubans struggle to survive on pocket-size government ration books as products dwindle
events.description: Cuba's pocket-size government ration book has been circulating for more than six decades

events.title: Serbian bird-watching group uses crowdfunding to buy and preserve woodland habitat
events.description: Nightingale’s Forest is a small woodland in northeastern Serbia that is home to a variety of bird and animal species who thrive on its moist habitat

events.title: Chinese pressure to exclude Taiwanese activists shuts down rights summit
events.description: New York-based group Access Now canceled the RightsCon summit in Zambia.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV encourages wealthy US Catholics to keep donating
events.description: Pope Leo XIV is encouraging some of the wealthiest U.S. Catholics to keep on giving to support his charitable works

events.title: Artemis II astronauts underscore our shared humanity, in historic UN visit
events.description: A month after completing their historic flyby of the Moon, the astronauts from NASA’s Artemis II mission landed at UN Headquarters in New York this week with a message that sounded like a reminder: humanity is capable of extraordinary things when it acts together.

events.title: Harvard scientist convicted of lying about China ties rebuilds brain-tech lab in China
events.description: Charles Lieber, a convicted former Harvard scientist, has rebuilt his brain-computer interface lab in Shenzhen, China, according to Reuters.

events.title: ICE lines up to boot illegal immigrant child sex predator after bogus asylum claim, early prison release
events.description: ICE agents want North Carolina police to turn over a Romanian illegal immigrant convicted of child sex crimes now facing possible deportation proceedings.

events.title: Bryan Kohberger's mother called him 'my angel' and insisted police made a 'really, really bad mistake': book
events.description: A new book reveals Bryan Kohberger's mother told FBI agents he was her "angel" after his 2022 arrest for the murders of four University of Idaho students.

events.title: WATCH: Driver smirks in mugshot after allegedly hitting group of cyclists in caught-on-camera road rage
events.description: An elderly man allegedly hit a group of cyclists in a caught-on-camera road rage incident in Georgia, leaving one rider with a fractured lower spine.

events.title: Trump’s 'Economic Fury' squeezes Iran — but can Tehran outlast the pressure?
events.description: Trump's escalating sanctions, naval blockade and financial enforcement against Iran test whether economic strain can force Iran's regime to bend.

events.title: Iran’s $800M oil smuggling scheme uses tankers posing as Iraqi ships to dodge blockade
events.description: Sanctioned tankers are allegedly disguising Iranian oil shipments as Iraqi cargo while Trump doubles down on the naval blockade to squeeze Tehran.

events.title: Record antisemitic incidents in Canada fuel criticism of Carney government response
events.description: Canada faces record antisemitic incidents in 2025 as critics say Prime Minister Mark Carney's government is failing to adequately address anti-Jewish hate.
